title: Cross-origin mount trust evaluation input/output (PRD-109)
description: Per PRD-109-R21 (the cross-origin mount trust evaluation algorithm). PRD-101-R11 requires consumers to evaluate origin trust before treating a mounted manifest as binding. This schema describes the structured input the trust-evaluation algorithm consumes and the verdict it emits. PRD-101/PRD-106 own the discovery and serving flows; PRD-109 owns the trust algorithm.

parent_manifest_origin.description: The origin (scheme://host[:port]) the parent manifest was fetched from.

mount_manifest_url.description: Absolute URL declared by the mount entry's manifest_url field.

consumer_allowlist.description: Consumer-configured list of origins (or origin patterns) the consumer trusts in addition to the parent origin. MAY be empty.

trusted.description: Final verdict. True iff scheme_equivalent AND (host_equivalent_or_allowlisted) AND mime_validated.

scheme_equivalent.description: True iff the mount's URL scheme matches the parent's. https→http downgrade MUST yield false.

host_equivalent_or_allowlisted.description: True iff the mount host equals the parent host OR appears in consumer_allowlist.

mime_validated.description: True iff the fetched mounted manifest's Content-Type matches application/act-manifest+json (per gap B5). A mismatch indicates a hostile or misconfigured mount and MUST yield false.

rejection_reason.description: Present and non-empty only when trusted == false.
